title: Docker Deep Dive
subtitle: How containers actually work — images, layers, the runtime, networking, and the production patterns that make Docker a foundation rather than a tool
description: A practical course on containers and Docker for engineers who use them every day but want to understand what's actually happening. Covers the mental model (namespaces, cgroups, union filesystems), image building (Dockerfiles, layers, multi-stage builds, optimization), the runtime (containerd, runc, what `docker run` actually does), networking (bridges, host, overlay, port mapping), volumes and data persistence, and production patterns (security, image scanning, registries, orchestration). Not a tutorial on first commands; a deep dive into the mechanics and disciplines.
audience: Developers and engineers who use Docker daily and want to understand what's underneath. Some Docker familiarity helps but not required for the conceptual lessons.
prerequisites: Basic Docker familiarity (you've run docker run)
Comfort with command line and Linux concepts
outcomes: Explain what containers actually are (namespaces, cgroups, union filesystems)
Write efficient Dockerfiles with proper layering and multi-stage builds
Understand the relationship between Docker, containerd, runc, and OCI
Configure Docker networking (bridge, host, overlay) confidently
Manage volumes and data persistence correctly
Secure containers (non-root, minimal images, scanning, signing)
Use registries effectively (ECR, GHCR, Docker Hub)
Avoid the common Docker mistakes that produce bloated images and security issues
